_meta.status: DRAFT — USER REVIEW REQUIRED before any edits to generation-content.ts
sources_consulted: Rennlist / community threads for conflict resolution
_meta.important: DO NOT auto-commit to generation-content.ts. Review every entry before applying. Pay particular attention to items with change_from_existing='update' or conflict=true.

variants.recommended_production_string: 20,395 worldwide (13,353 Coupe; 4,802 Cab; 1,329 Targa; 911 Jubilee Coupé)

sources.value_cited: 20,395 Carrera 4 total (13,353 Coupe + 4,802 Cab + 1,329 Targa + 911 Jubilee)

variants.notes: Jubilee Coupé (911 units) is the 30 Jahre/30th Anniversary variant built on Carrera 4. Wikipedia lists it within the Carrera 4 row.

variants.recommended_production_string: 34,398 worldwide (18,219 Coupe; 11,013 Cab; 3,534 Targa; 1,532 Turbo-look Cab; 936 Speedster)

sources.value_cited: 34,398 Carrera 2 total broken down by body style

variants.notes: Speedster (936 narrow-body) is included in the Carrera 2 row in the Wikipedia production table. The 15 Exclusiv widebody Speedsters are separate and not included in this 34,398 figure — they are classified as Turbo variants.
Does not include the Turbo S Leichtbau (86 units) or the Turbo 3.6 S Flachbau/Package (93 units) which are listed as separate variants.

Existing figure confirmed. Note: the gen-content description says 1,910 Lightweights specifically — that sub-figure is not independently confirmed here.

researched_figures.us_north_america: 701 (US market car — no significant ROW allocation)

variants.notes: Existing figure confirmed.

researched_figures.notes_on_split: Wikipedia table combines all 272 PS (pre-Varioram) cars regardless of year. This covers MY1994 (ROW) and MY1995 (US) through the MY1996 Varioram introduction.

variants.notes: Note: The Wikipedia table uses engine output (272 vs 285 PS) not marketing year to distinguish pre-Varioram from Varioram. The gen-content.ts describes pre-Varioram as 'MY1995 only' from a US perspective, but worldwide it includes MY1994 ROW cars. This figure covers all 272-PS production.

Counter-intuitive that the 3-year Varioram run (MY1996-1998) produced fewer narrow-body Carreras than the 2-year pre-Varioram run. This may reflect the Carrera 4S and Carrera S drawing buyers away from the narrow-body C2 in the Varioram years, plus a shorter production run (MY1998 ended March 1998).

variants.recommended_production_string: ~7,166 worldwide (2,884 + 1,860 Coupes; 1,284 + 1,138 Cabs across both specs)

sources.value_cited: C4 272PS: Coupe 2,884; Cab 1,284. C4 285PS: Coupe 1,860; Cab 1,138

variants.notes: No Tiptronic option on C4 (6MT only per gen-content). The lower Carrera 4 numbers vs Carrera 2 reflect the narrower market for AWD-only, manual-only narrow-body.
Existing gen-content only had US figure (~2,079). Worldwide total is 6,948. US figure not reverified; flagging as an update to add the worldwide total.
Existing gen-content had only US figure. Worldwide total confirmed at 3,714.

researched_figures.conflict_note: Wikipedia production table states 4,583; Wikipedia text section sums year-by-year to 4,619

variants.notes: FLAGGED: Existing gen-content figure (~5,400) is higher than both Wikipedia figures (4,583/4,619). The ~5,400 figure may have included Carrera 4S Targa-look or been an estimate. Recommend updating to ~4,620 based on Wikipedia year-by-year data. The two Wikipedia figures (4,583 vs 4,619) reflect a minor table vs text discrepancy.

Existing ~6,000 rounds up from 5,978. Minor update to be precise. Note: Wikipedia Turbo figure 5,978 includes ALL 993 Turbos (std + Turbo S). The Turbo S (345 units) should be subtracted to get pure Turbo count: 5,978 − 345 = 5,633 standard Turbos.

researched_figures.us_north_america: 181 (not independently confirmed from a second source)

sources.value_cited: 345 worldwide confirmed; 181 US noted as community consensus

variants.notes: 345 worldwide is well-established across sources. US figure of 181 is community consensus but not confirmed from an independent second source; medium confidence on the US split. Note: 277 sometimes cited in UK sources refers to UK Turbo S total, not global Turbo S.

conflict_breakdown.57: Wikipedia cites 57 road-legal variants — possibly counting only federalized/homologation-spec cars
conflict_breakdown.194: Marque sources and gen-content — likely the correct total including all customer road cars across all model years
conflict_breakdown.223: p-car.com production registry — may include race-prepared customer competition cars not technically road cars

variants.recommended_production_string: ~194 road cars worldwide (source conflict: Wikipedia cites 57; p-car.com cites 223)
variants.notes: FLAGGED CONFLICT: Gen-content already acknowledges this conflict ('Wikipedia cites 57, marque sources cite 194'). The ~194 figure is widely accepted by the specialist community. The Wikipedia 57 may refer only to true road cars stripped of all competition equipment. The 223 p-car.com figure likely includes all customer competition builds. RECOMMEND: Keep ~194 road cars in gen-content; flag as disputed. Do not change without additional specialist source.

996.1.generation_total_notes: Wikipedia gives 175,262 for entire 996 production. 996.1 years (1997-2001): ~85,556 from year-by-year table.

variants.notes: Note: The gen-content 'units_produced' field currently says '~37,750 Carrera Coupes worldwide (all 996 combined)' which appears to be a rough per-generation estimate. The Supercar Nostalgia 996.1 figure alone gives 31,688 Coupes, and the full 996 total would be much higher.

Do NOT update gen-content until this is resolved with a dedicated source check. The 991.2 Turbo S figure from search was 10,079 worldwide.

982-boxster.notes: Porsche does not publish per-variant production figures for the 982/718 generation. Wikipedia article confirmed to have no production data. Production of all 718 variants ended October 2025.

982-cayman.notes: Same situation as 982 Boxster — no public per-variant production data. Production ended October 2025.
